api/client/javascript/src/client.purpose: Generated TypeScript SDK client implementing the full OpenMeter admin API surface. Each domain resource is a class wrapping an openapi-fetch Client<paths> with typed routes, sub-resource composition, and uniform error/date handling via transformResponse.

patterns.name: Resource class wrapping openapi-fetch
patterns.description: Every domain file exports one or more classes. Each class takes Client<paths, `${string}/${string}`> in its constructor and exposes async methods that call this.client.GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH with typed path literals, then return transformResponse(resp). Never access resp.data directly.

patterns.name: Nested sub-resource classes as public fields
patterns.description: Compound domains (Billing, Apps, Customers, Notifications, Plans, Entitlements) expose sub-resources as public class fields initialized in the constructor. Each sub-class takes the same client reference.

patterns.name: Types always from schemas.ts via operations/paths
patterns.description: All domain types (input shapes, path params, query params) come from './schemas.js' imports — either named types or operations['operationName']['parameters']['path'|'query'] and requestBody['content']['application/json']. Never invent local type shapes.
patterns.example:
import type { AddonCreate, operations, paths } from './schemas.js'
public async update(id: operations['updateAddon']['parameters']['path']['id'], addon: operations['updateAddon']['requestBody']['content']['application/json'], options?: RequestOptions)

patterns.name: RequestOptions on every public method
patterns.description: Every public method accepts options?: RequestOptions as its last parameter and spreads it into the fetch call with ...options. RequestOptions = Pick<RequestInit, 'signal'> defined in common.ts.

patterns.name: OpenMeter root class registers every resource
patterns.description: index.ts creates the openapi-fetch client with date-encoding querySerializer and optional Bearer auth, then instantiates every resource class as a public field. Adding a new resource requires: (1) new domain file, (2) import in index.ts, (3) public field declaration, (4) constructor instantiation.
patterns.example:
this.client = createClient<paths>({ ...config, querySerializer: (q) => createQuerySerializer({ array: { explode: true, style: 'form' }, object: { explode: true, style: 'deepObject' } })(encodeDates(q)) })
this.addons = new Addons(this.client)

patterns.name: Events ingest uses application/cloudevents-batch+json
patterns.description: events.ts special-cases the ingest method: normalizes single/array input to array, applies setDefaultsForEvent (id, source, specversion, time defaults), and sends Content-Type: application/cloudevents-batch+json. The list method uses standard application/json.
patterns.example: const resp = await this.client.POST('/api/v1/events', { body, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/cloudevents-batch+json' }, ...options })

patterns.name: Explicit return type casts for typed responses
patterns.description: When the generic transformResponse return type is too broad, methods cast the result to the exact operations response type. See meters.ts query() and queryPost().
patterns.example: return transformResponse(resp) as operations['queryMeter']['responses']['200']['content']['application/json']

key_file_guides.role: Root OpenMeter class and Config type. Creates the openapi-fetch client with date-encoding querySerializer (encodeDates + form/deepObject serialization), optional Bearer token, and instantiates all resource classes as public fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a resource class file without registering it as a public field AND calling new in the constructor — the instance will be unreachable. Config type requires apiKey only when baseUrl is 'https://openmeter.cloud'.

key_file_guides.role: transformResponse (throws HTTPError on 4xx/5xx, recursively decodes ISO date strings to Date objects), decodeDates, encodeDates (recursive Date→ISO string used in query serializer).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never skip transformResponse; direct resp.data access misses date decoding and error handling entirely.

key_file_guides.role: Defines RequestOptions (Pick<RequestInit, 'signal'>), HTTPError class with fromResponse factory (reads application/problem+json for structured detail), and isHTTPError type guard.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HTTPError.fromResponse checks Content-Type: application/problem+json to extract problem.detail as the error message. Use isHTTPError to distinguish API errors from network failures.

key_file_guides.role: Generated file exporting paths, operations, and all domain types. Source of truth for all type shapes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerate via make gen-api. Any manual edit will be overwritten.

key_file_guides.role: Most complex resource: Billing class with three sub-classes (BillingProfiles, BillingInvoices, BillingCustomers). BillingInvoices exposes state-machine actions: advance, approve, retry, void, recalculateTax, snapshotQuantities, simulate, createLineItems, invoicePendingLines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Most invoice actions take only path params (no body), but void requires body: VoidInvoiceActionInput and simulate/createLineItems/invoicePendingLines require body. Don't omit required bodies.

key_file_guides.role: Customers class with four sub-classes: CustomerApps, CustomerEntitlements (v1 /api/v1), CustomerEntitlementsV2 (/api/v2), CustomerStripe.
key_file_guides.watch_for: customers.entitlementsV1 hits /api/v1 paths; customers.entitlements hits /api/v2 paths — they are different API versions, do not mix when adding methods.

key_file_guides.role: Events.ingest normalizes input, sets CloudEvent defaults (id via node:crypto or fallback UUID, source='@openmeter/sdk', specversion='1.0', time=now), sends as cloudevents-batch+json.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use Content-Type: application/cloudevents-batch+json for POST /api/v1/events. UUID generation tries node:crypto first and falls back to Math.random-based UUID for browser compatibility.

key_file_guides.role: Vitest tests using @fetch-mock/vitest. Shows correct test pattern: fetchMock.mockReset() in beforeEach, route assertions with fetchMock.callHistory.done().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Date params must be serialized to ISO strings in mock route query matchers — the SDK encodes dates before sending. The test verifies this encoding behavior.

anti_patterns: Defining domain type shapes locally instead of importing from './schemas.js' — all shapes come from the generated file
Accessing resp.data directly without calling transformResponse — loses date decoding and HTTPError throwing on 4xx/5xx
Adding a resource class but not registering it as a public field AND constructor instantiation in OpenMeter (index.ts)
Manually editing schemas.ts — it is code-generated and will be overwritten by make gen-api
Omitting options?: RequestOptions from a public method or not spreading ...options into the fetch call — prevents AbortSignal propagation

decisions.decision: openapi-fetch with typed paths/operations from generated schemas.ts
decisions.rationale: Compile-time type-checking of route paths, path params, query params, and request/response bodies. Any API change in TypeSpec caught at TypeScript compile time after regeneration, preventing SDK drift.

decisions.decision: Date values encoded (Date→ISO string) in query serializer and decoded (ISO→Date) in transformResponse
decisions.rationale: OpenAPI dates are strings; SDK callers expect JS Date objects. Centralizing encode/decode in utils.ts ensures consistent Date handling across all endpoints without per-method boilerplate.

decisions.decision: Each domain has its own file; index.ts is the only file that imports all of them
decisions.rationale: Tree-shaking friendly — consumers who import individual classes only pull in their file. index.ts provides the convenience-bundled OpenMeter class for callers wanting the full client.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new resource class for a new domain 'widgets' with list and create methods

api/client/javascript/src/portal.purpose: Restricted portal-scoped client for end-customer access. Exposes a single OpenMeter class authenticated via portalToken (required, not optional) that provides only the portal meter query endpoint — intentionally a much smaller surface than the full admin client.

patterns.name: Imports utilities from parent client, not redefined
patterns.description: All utility logic (RequestOptions, paths/operations types, transformResponse, encodeDates) is imported from '../client/common.js', '../client/schemas.js', and '../client/utils.js'. This file adds only portal-specific wiring.
patterns.example:
import type { RequestOptions } from '../client/common.js'
import type { operations, paths } from '../client/schemas.js'
import { encodeDates, transformResponse } from '../client/utils.js'

patterns.name: portalToken replaces apiKey in Config
patterns.description: Config type requires portalToken: string (not optional). Auth header is always `Bearer ${config.portalToken}`. The same querySerializer shape (form explode, deepObject) as the main client must be kept in sync.
patterns.example: export type Config = Pick<ClientOptions, 'baseUrl' | 'headers' | 'fetch' | 'Request' | 'requestInitExt'> & { portalToken: string }

patterns.name: Accept: application/json header on meter query
patterns.description: The portal meter query endpoint can return CSV if Accept header is not set. Always pass Accept: application/json explicitly, and cast the return type to the exact operations response type.
patterns.example:
const resp = await this.client.GET('/api/v1/portal/meters/{meterSlug}/query', { headers: { Accept: 'application/json' }, params: { path: { meterSlug }, query }, ...options })
return transformResponse(resp) as operations['queryPortalMeter']['responses']['200']['content']['application/json']

key_file_guides.role: Entire portal SDK in one file. Creates openapi-fetch client, wires Bearer portalToken, exposes single query() method for /api/v1/portal/meters/{meterSlug}/query.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must pass Accept: application/json explicitly on the meter query — endpoint returns CSV otherwise. querySerializer shape must stay in sync with main client index.ts.

anti_patterns: Adding admin-level endpoints to the portal client — this client is intentionally restricted to portal-scoped access only
Redefining transformResponse or encodeDates instead of importing from '../client/utils.js'
Making portalToken optional in Config — portal requests always require authentication
Omitting Accept: application/json on the meter query — endpoint falls back to CSV response

decisions.decision: Portal client is a separate minimal class rather than a subset configuration of the main OpenMeter client
decisions.rationale: Prevents accidentally exposing admin endpoints to end-customer portal tokens and makes the restricted surface explicit at the type level with no risk of scope creep.

api/client/javascript/src/react.purpose: Thin React integration layer providing a context/provider/hook trio (OpenMeterContext, OpenMeterProvider, useOpenMeter) so React apps can inject and consume a portal OpenMeter client anywhere in the component tree. This is a single file; all SDK logic lives in ../portal/.

patterns.name: Re-export portal surface wholesale
patterns.description: context.tsx does `export * from '../portal/index.js'` so all portal SDK types and functions are available from the react import path. Consumers import from '@openmeter/sdk/react' and get everything.
patterns.example: export * from '../portal/index.js'

patterns.name: Null-initialized context with undefined-throws hook
patterns.description: OpenMeterContext default is null (unauthenticated state). useOpenMeter throws only if context is undefined (outside provider). Callers check for null themselves to handle unauthenticated state.

patterns.name: 'use client' directive at top
patterns.description: File is marked 'use client' for Next.js App Router compatibility. Any addition to this file must preserve this directive at the very first line.
patterns.example: 'use client'

key_file_guides.role: Entire folder. Exports OpenMeterContext, OpenMeterProvider, useOpenMeter, and re-exports all portal SDK symbols.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic or API call methods here. All SDK behaviour belongs in ../portal/. New exports should come via re-export from ../portal/, not defined locally. Do not remove 'use client'.

anti_patterns: Defining SDK methods or API call logic directly in context.tsx instead of delegating to ../portal/
Removing the 'use client' directive — breaks Next.js App Router
Creating a second context or provider for different API concerns — extend the OpenMeter type in portal/ instead
Throwing inside useOpenMeter when value is null — null is a valid unauthenticated state, only undefined should throw

decisions.decision: Context default is null, not a noop object
decisions.rationale: Forces callers to handle the unauthenticated state explicitly rather than silently calling noop methods that appear to work but do nothing.

decisions.decision: All portal exports are re-exported from this entry point
decisions.rationale: Consumers importing from the react path get the full portal SDK surface without a second import statement, keeping usage ergonomic while preserving a single source of truth in ../portal/.

code_examples.scenario: Wrap app with provider and consume client in a component

api/client/javascript/src/zod.purpose: Auto-generated Zod validation schemas for all OpenMeter API query parameters and request bodies, produced by orval from the OpenAPI spec. Exports typed Zod objects and constant defaults/regex patterns per operation — never hand-edit this file.

patterns.name: Generated by orval — do not edit
patterns.description: index.ts carries the 'Generated by orval v8.7.0 — Do not edit manually.' header. All schemas are regenerated from the OpenAPI spec. Any manual edit will be overwritten on the next make gen-api.
patterns.example:
/**
 * Generated by orval v8.7.0 🍺
 * Do not edit manually.
 */

patterns.name: Constants exported before each Zod schema
patterns.description: Each operation exports named constants for defaults, min/max values, and RegExp patterns before the Zod schema object (e.g. listAddonsQueryPageDefault = 1, listAddonsQueryKeyItemRegExp). These constants are referenced inside the zod.object() calls.

patterns.name: zod.coerce for all scalar fields
patterns.description: Query parameters use zod.coerce.string(), zod.coerce.number(), zod.coerce.boolean() to handle URL string coercion automatically. This matches how form-encoded query strings arrive in validated contexts.
patterns.example: includeDeleted: zod.coerce.boolean().default(listAddonsQueryIncludeDeletedDefault)

key_file_guides.role: Single generated file exporting all Zod schemas for API query param and body validation. Exports both the constants (defaults, regexps, min/max) and the Zod schema objects per operation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT. Import and use these schemas for server-side validation of incoming query parameters. The naming convention is <OperationPascalCase>QueryParams or <OperationPascalCase>Body.

anti_patterns: Manually editing index.ts — it is regenerated by orval from the OpenAPI spec on make gen-api
Redefining Zod schemas locally when the generated schemas already cover the operation
Using the raw generated constants (defaults/regexps) in application logic — they are implementation details of the schema and subject to regeneration

decisions.decision: Zod schemas generated by orval from the same OpenAPI spec that generates TypeScript types
decisions.rationale: Keeps runtime validation (Zod) and compile-time types (TypeScript) in sync with the TypeSpec source of truth. Single make gen-api updates both.

api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/a.purpose: Async (asyncio) entry point for the OpenMeter Python SDK — exposes OpenMeterClient with AsyncPipelineClient and one attribute per resource group. All async I/O is handled here; request construction is entirely delegated to the sync operations layer under the parent package.

patterns.name: AsyncPipelineClient injection with fixed policy chain
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient.__init__ builds AsyncPipelineClient from an ordered policy list (headers → user_agent → proxy → ContentDecodePolicy → retry → authentication → logging) and injects (self._client, self._config, self._serialize, self._deserialize) as positional args into every Operations class.
patterns.example: self.customers = CustomersOperations(self._client, self._config, self._serialize, self._deserialize)

patterns.name: Async context manager protocol
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient implements __aenter__ / __aexit__ and close() for use as `async with OpenMeterClient(...) as client`. __aenter__ delegates to self._client.__aenter__() and returns self.
patterns.example: async def __aenter__(self) -> Self: await self._client.__aenter__(); return self

patterns.name: send_request URL formatting via deepcopy
patterns.description: send_request deep-copies the HttpRequest and formats the {endpoint} path placeholder via self._client.format_url() before delegating to self._client.send_request — never pass a raw URL directly.
patterns.example: request_copy.url = self._client.format_url(request_copy.url, **path_format_arguments)

patterns.name: _patch.py customization hook
patterns.description: All hand-written customizations belong in _patch.py. __init__.py imports _patch_all and calls patch_sdk() after importing the generated client. Never edit _client.py directly — it is generated and overwritten on SDK regeneration.
patterns.example: from ._patch import __all__ as _patch_all; from ._patch import *; _patch_sdk()

patterns.name: AsyncRetryPolicy in configuration
patterns.description: _configuration.py uses policies.AsyncRetryPolicy (not the sync RetryPolicy) for the retry_policy field. authentication_policy defaults to None — callers must supply it via kwargs.
patterns.example: self.retry_policy = kwargs.get('retry_policy') or policies.AsyncRetryPolicy(**kwargs)

key_file_guides.role: Defines OpenMeterClient: builds AsyncPipelineClient, owns the policy chain, and instantiates every Operations attribute as a named instance attribute.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated file — do not hand-edit. Duplicate attribute assignments (e.g. self.subjects assigned twice) are a codegen artifact; do not fix manually. Adding new resource groups requires SDK regeneration.

key_file_guides.role: Holds OpenMeterClientConfiguration: stores endpoint, sdk_moniker, polling_interval, and all policy instances consumed by _client.py.
key_file_guides.watch_for: authentication_policy defaults to None (no auth); callers must pass it via kwargs. Use AsyncRetryPolicy here, not the sync RetryPolicy.

key_file_guides.role: Empty hook file for safe hand-written customizations; patch_sdk() is called at every import.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any logic added here must be idempotent — it runs at every import. Keep __all__ updated if adding public symbols.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point: re-exports OpenMeterClient and merges _patch_all into __all__.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The TYPE_CHECKING guard around `from ._patch import *` is intentional for linters — do not remove it.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing _client.py — it is generated and will be overwritten on the next SDK regeneration cycle
Instantiating Operations classes directly rather than accessing them as client attributes (bypasses shared serializer/deserializer injection)
Passing a bare URL string to send_request without formatting the {endpoint} placeholder via self._client.format_url()
Raising or re-raising exceptions on streaming error responses before calling `await response.read()` — leaks the connection
Adding business logic or retry logic inside _patch.py instead of in the Operations layer

decisions.decision: All request-building logic lives in the sync operations/_operations.py build_* functions; async operations only handle execution and response deserialization.
decisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating URL/param construction and keeps sync and async layers consistent from a single generated source.

decisions.decision: OpenMeterClientConfiguration is a separate class injected into every Operations constructor rather than passed as raw kwargs.
decisions.rationale: Centralises policy instantiation (retry, auth, logging) and makes the entire policy chain replaceable at construction time without subclassing the client.

code_examples.scenario: Async context manager usage with custom auth and a raw send_request call
code_examples.code:
from corehttp.runtime.pipeline import BearerTokenCredentialPolicy
from openmeter._generated.aio import OpenMeterClient
from corehttp.rest import HttpRequest

async def main():
    async with OpenMeterClient(
        endpoint="https://openmeter.cloud",
        authentication_policy=BearerTokenCredentialPolicy(credential, "api")
    ) as client:
        meters = await client.meters.list()

        # Raw request — must use send_request to format {endpoint}
        req = HttpRequest("GET", "{endpoint}/api/v1/meters")
        resp = await client.send_request(req)
